title: Compare Two Related Measurements Without Assuming Normality — wilcoxon_test
text: normally distributed paired data: Use paired t-test instead independent groups: Use mann_whitney() instead

title: Compare Two Related Measurements Without Assuming Normality — wilcoxon_test
text: SPSS NPAR TESTS ignores WEIGHT , weighted results SPSS reference. weighted variant R-frequency-weight extension reduces exactly unweighted test weights equal 1 (enforced internal invariance suite); see vignette("spss-compatibility") validation status. 3+ related measurements: Use friedman_test() instead

title: Compare Two Related Measurements Without Assuming Normality — wilcoxon_test
text: Wilcoxon, F. (1945). Individual comparisons ranking methods. Biometrics Bulletin, 1(6), 80-83. Fritz, C. O., Morris, P. E., & Richler, J. J. (2012). Effect size estimates: current use, calculations, interpretation. Journal Experimental Psychology: General, 141(1), 2.

title: Compare Two Related Measurements Without Assuming Normality — wilcoxon_test
code: # Load required packages and data library(dplyr) data(survey_data)  # Compare trust in government vs trust in media survey_data %>%   wilcoxon_test(x = trust_government, y = trust_media) #> Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test: trust_media - trust_government #>   Z = -5.097, p < 0.001 ***, r = 0.123 (small), N = 2227 #> Use summary() for detailed output.  # Weighted analysis survey_data %>%   wilcoxon_test(x = trust_government, y = trust_media,                 weights = sampling_weight) #> Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test: trust_media - trust_government [Weighted] #>   Z = -5.035, p < 0.001 ***, r = 0.121 (small), N = 2242 #> Use summary() for detailed output.  # Grouped analysis (separate test per region) survey_data %>%   group_by(region) %>%   wilcoxon_test(x = trust_government, y = trust_media) #> [region = 1] #> Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test: trust_media - trust_government #>   Z = -2.727, p = 0.006 **, r = 0.147 (small), N = 435 #> [region = 2] #> Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test: trust_media - trust_government #>   Z = -4.346, p < 0.001 ***, r = 0.117 (small), N = 1792 #> Use summary() for detailed output.

what: Export Data to SPSS Format — write_spss
title: Export Data to SPSS Format — write_spss
text: Writes data frame SPSS .sav file, preserving variable labels, value labels, user-defined missing values. exporting data imported read_spss() (tag_na = TRUE), tagged NAs automatically converted back SPSS user-defined missing values, enabling full roundtrip fidelity.

title: Export Data to SPSS Format — write_spss
code: write_spss(data, path, compress = c("byte", "none", "zsav"))

title: Export Data to SPSS Format — write_spss
text: data data frame export. Columns class haven_labelled labels missing value metadata written .sav file. path Path output file. Must end .sav .zsav. compress Compression type. One "byte" (default, byte-level compression), "none" (compression), "zsav" (zlib compression, requires SPSS v21+).

title: Export Data to SPSS Format — write_spss
text: Invisibly returns file path.

title: Export Data to SPSS Format — write_spss
text: Data imported via read_spss() stores SPSS user-defined missing values tagged NAs na_tag_map attribute mapping tag characters original codes (e.g., -9, -8). write_spss() reverses process: tagged NAs converted back original numeric codes, SPSS user-defined missing value specification reconstructed exported .sav file missing value definitions original.

title: Export Data to SPSS Format — write_spss
text: exporting data originally imported Stata SAS (native extended missing values like .-.z .-.Z), represented SPSS user-defined missing values. case, written system missing (regular NA) warning.

title: Export Data to SPSS Format — write_spss
text: Use write_spss() : Need export processed survey data back SPSS format Want preserve user-defined missing value definitions Need roundtrip fidelity: read_spss() -> processing -> write_spss()

title: Export Data to SPSS Format — write_spss
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Roundtrip: read SPSS, process, write back data <- read_spss("survey.sav") data_clean <- data[data$age >= 18, ] write_spss(data_clean, "survey_adults.sav")  # Export with zlib compression (smaller file, requires SPSS v21+) write_spss(data, "survey_compressed.zsav", compress = "zsav") } # }

what: Export Data to Stata Format — write_stata
title: Export Data to Stata Format — write_stata
text: Writes data frame Stata .dta file, preserving variable labels, value labels, missing value types. Tagged NAs source format (SPSS, Stata, SAS) written Stata extended missing values (..z).

title: Export Data to Stata Format — write_stata
code: write_stata(data, path, version = 14)

title: Export Data to Stata Format — write_stata
text: data data frame export. Columns class haven_labelled labels written .dta file. path Path output file. Must end .dta. version Stata file version use. Supported values: 8-15. Default 14 (Stata 14/15, supports Unicode). Use 13 compatibility older Stata versions.

title: Export Data to Stata Format — write_stata
text: Invisibly returns file path.

title: Export Data to Stata Format — write_stata
text: Stata natively supports 27 extended missing values: ..z . (system missing). exporting data tagged NAs: Stata (read_stata()): Native extended missing values preserved full roundtrip. SPSS (read_spss()): Tagged NAs written Stata extended missing values (., .b, etc.). original SPSS numeric codes (e.g., -9, -8) preserved Stata file, distinct missing value types labels . SAS (read_sas(), read_xpt()): SAS special missing values mapped Stata equivalents.

title: Export Data to Stata Format — write_stata
text: Stata supports 27 distinct missing types per variable. SPSS data 27 tagged NA types single variable may lose distinctions (though extremely rare practice).

title: Export Data to Stata Format — write_stata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Roundtrip: read Stata, process, write back data <- read_stata("survey.dta") data_clean <- data[data$age >= 18, ] write_stata(data_clean, "survey_adults.dta")  # Cross-format: SPSS to Stata data <- read_spss("survey.sav") write_stata(data, "survey.dta")  # Stata 13 compatibility write_stata(data, "survey_v13.dta", version = 13) } # }

what: Export Data to Excel with Label Support — write_xlsx
title: Export Data to Excel with Label Support — write_xlsx
text: Writes data Excel (.xlsx) file support variable labels, value labels, tagged NA metadata. exporting labelled survey data, "Labels" reference sheet automatically included reviewers can look numeric code means – even without R access. Three input types supported: Data frame: Exports data plus "Labels" reference sheet variable labels, value labels, missing value codes. Codebook object: Exports codebook appears HTML viewer – values, value labels, frequencies stacked inside cell, tagged NAs separated line, overview sheet dataset metadata. Named list: list element becomes separate sheet. Elements can data frames, frequency() results, codebook() results – types can mixed freely. data frame elements, combined "Labels" sheet appended.

title: Export Data to Excel with Label Support — write_xlsx
code: write_xlsx(x, file, ...)  # S3 method for class 'frequency' write_xlsx(x, file, overwrite = TRUE, ...)

title: Export Data to Excel with Label Support — write_xlsx
text: x Object export: data frame, codebook() result, named list data frames. file Path output .xlsx file. Must end .xlsx. ... Additional arguments passed methods: labels (data.frame list methods) Include "Labels" reference sheet? Default: TRUE frequencies (codebook method) Include per-variable frequency sheets? Default: FALSE overwrite Overwrite existing file? Default: TRUE.

title: Export Data to Excel with Label Support — write_xlsx
text: Invisibly returns file path.

title: Export Data to Excel with Label Support — write_xlsx
text: "Labels" sheet provides complete lookup table variable value labels data, structured long format. "Type" column distinguishes regular value labels ("valid") tagged missing value labels ("missing"), making easy filter Excel. Data values written underlying numeric character codes (label text), preserving original coding scheme.

title: Export Data to Excel with Label Support — write_xlsx
text: codebook export reproduces HTML codebook layout: variable occupies one row, values, value labels, frequencies stacked within cells using line breaks. Tagged NAs shown separator line, matching visual style codebook() RStudio Viewer.

title: Export Data to Excel with Label Support — write_xlsx
text: Use write_xlsx() : Need share survey data non-R users need label context Want export codebook manual review documentation Need combine multiple tables (data, codebook, frequencies) one Excel file

title: Export Data to Excel with Label Support — write_xlsx
text: write_xlsx(frequency): Export frequency tables Excel Exports one frequency tables single Excel sheet, mirroring console print output. variable gets header row, stats summary, column headers, data rows, total rows. Multiple variables separated 3 blank rows.

title: Export Data to Excel with Label Support — write_xlsx
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Export data with automatic label reference sheet write_xlsx(survey_data, "survey_export.xlsx")  # Export a codebook (reproduces HTML layout in Excel) codebook(survey_data) |> write_xlsx("codebook.xlsx")  # Export a codebook with per-variable frequency sheets codebook(survey_data) |> write_xlsx("codebook_full.xlsx", frequencies = TRUE)  # Export frequency tables (single or multiple variables) frequency(survey_data, gender) |> write_xlsx("freq.xlsx") frequency(survey_data, gender, life_satisfaction, region) |>   write_xlsx("freq_multi.xlsx")  # Multi-sheet export (mixed types: data frames, frequencies, codebooks) write_xlsx(   list(     "Frequencies" = frequency(survey_data, gender, life_satisfaction),     "Codebook"    = codebook(survey_data),     "Data"        = survey_data   ),   "combined.xlsx" ) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single variable frequency(survey_data, gender) |> write_xlsx("freq.xlsx")  # Multiple variables on one sheet frequency(survey_data, gender, life_satisfaction, region) |>   write_xlsx("freq_multi.xlsx") } # }

what: Export Data to SAS Transport Format — write_xpt
title: Export Data to SAS Transport Format — write_xpt
text: Writes data frame SAS transport file (.xpt), preserving variable labels missing value types. Tagged NAs source format (SPSS, Stata, SAS) written SAS special missing values (..Z, ._).

title: Export Data to SAS Transport Format — write_xpt
code: write_xpt(data, path, version = 5, name = NULL)

title: Export Data to SAS Transport Format — write_xpt
text: data data frame export. path Path output file. Must end .xpt. version SAS transport file version. Either 5 (default, SAS Transport v5, compatible) 8 (SAS Transport v8, supports longer variable names). name Member name dataset within transport file. NULL, derived file name. Maximum 8 characters version 5.

title: Export Data to SAS Transport Format — write_xpt
text: Invisibly returns file path.

title: Export Data to SAS Transport Format — write_xpt
text: SAS supports 28 special missing values: . (system missing), ..Z, ._. exporting data tagged NAs: SAS (read_sas(), read_xpt()): Native special missing values preserved full roundtrip. SPSS (read_spss()): Tagged NAs written SAS special missing values. original SPSS numeric codes (e.g., -9, -8) preserved, distinct missing value types . Stata (read_stata()): Stata extended missing values mapped SAS equivalents.

title: Export Data to SAS Transport Format — write_xpt
text: SAS transport files store value labels. data value labels, consider using write_spss() write_xlsx() instead, preserve full label metadata. Variable labels preserved.

title: Export Data to SAS Transport Format — write_xpt
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Roundtrip: read SAS transport, process, write back data <- read_xpt("survey.xpt") data_clean <- data[data$age >= 18, ] write_xpt(data_clean, "survey_adults.xpt")  # Cross-format: SPSS to SAS transport data <- read_spss("survey.sav") write_xpt(data, "survey.xpt") } # }

title: mariposa 0.6.14
text: Codebook robustness (theme: codebook() survives real-world data says shows). stress test codebook stack (metadata extraction, console print/summary, HTML builder, xlsx export) surfaced batch crashes, silent data errors, display leaks; release fixes adds view argument side-effect control.

title: mariposa 0.6.14
text: Variables value labels variable label longer show fake label like “1 | 2 | 3”: attr(x, "label") partially matched labels attribute; label reads now use exact = TRUE. “Variables labels” count excludes variables accordingly. codebook() longer errors inline data expressions (e.g. codebook(data.frame(...)) spanning multiple deparse lines); long expressions collapse generic dataset name “data”. List columns longer crash frequency computation: skipped warning (“list column … skipped - supported codebooks”) data frame consisting list columns aborts clear error. HTML builder appends missing-value rows (codes, labels, counts) range-displayed variables — previously -user-missing high-cardinality variable silently lost (xlsx export already correctly); NA frequencies computed even high-cardinality/range-displayed variables; print(summary(cb)) gains “Missing values:” section (codes, labels, counts) — show_na -op console layer .

title: mariposa 0.6.14
text: Central display formatting numeric values: empirical values ranges longer leak 15-digit doubles scientific notation console/HTML/xlsx output. Fractional values show 4 significant digits (“0.3333”), whole numbers keep integer look (“1”), tiny values expanded (“0.00000001”, never “1e-08”). Frequency matching unaffected: display strings raw matching keys carried separately (empirical_values vs. new empirical_keys). percentage effective-n columns (prc, valid_prc, cum_prc, n_eff) write_xlsx(cb, frequencies = TRUE) sheets rounded 2 decimals. max_values (single integer >= 1) max_len (single integer >= 4) validated front clear error. Factor levels now respect max_values truncate “… (N )” note used character values. Range displays show cardinality: “18 - 95 (78 distinct)” instead just “18 - 95”. file = nonexistent directory aborts early, naming missing directory. Polish: “1 variable” / “1 observation” pluralization console HTML subtitle; long character values truncated max_len “…” (raw values still drive frequency matching); zero-row data frames say “(observations)” instead “(missing)”.

title: mariposa 0.6.14
text: New view argument codebook() (default: interactive()): controls whether HTML codebook opens RStudio Viewer. view = FALSE suppresses Viewer side effect entirely; writing via file = unaffected. compact print() advertises Viewer actually opened (result gains viewed flag).

title: mariposa 0.6.13
text: McDonald’s omega (theme: reliability() learns second reliability coefficient). reliability() now reports McDonald’s omega alongside Cronbach’s alpha — new statistic within existing function, hence PATCH per clarified versioning policy.

title: mariposa 0.6.13
text: omega — raw/total omega covariance metric (analogous raw alpha), reported “McDonald’s Omega”; omega_std — standardized omega correlation metric (analogous standardized alpha); omega_if_deleted — new column item_total, refitting one-factor model per deleted item (NA reduced scale fewer 3 items, model unidentified). Scales fewer 3 items get NA omega fields plus warning (alpha unaffected); non-convergent factor fits degrade NA factanal message. compact print() shows omega next alpha, summary() adds omega rows Reliability Statistics block omega column Item-Total table.

title: mariposa 0.6.13
text: McDonald’s omega Tier 4 (Internal, R-) now: SPSS v27+ offers omega RELIABILITY, IBM’s algorithm documentation publicly retrievable SPSS v29 reference run exists yet. pending reference run prepared .claude/spss-syntax-omega-references.sps (expected values included); lands, omega guarded parameter-recovery test simulated congeneric data, exact cross-checks manual factanal computation, cross-checks psych::omega() lavaan/semTools one-factor CFA (tests/testthat/test-reliability-omega.R), w == 1 block weights-invariance suite. help page carries Tier-4 disclosure; compatibility vignette flags omega Internal (Tier 4). psych, lavaan, semTools added Suggests (cross-check tests ; gated skip_if_not_installed()).

title: mariposa 0.6.12
text: Weighted-rank correctness accurate claims (theme: weighted rank family says exactly ). Two formula errors weighted rank statistics fixed package-wide invariance suite now guards every weighted entry point; alongside, user-facing claim surface (README, DESCRIPTION, help pages, compatibility vignette) realigned validation suite actually covers.

title: mariposa 0.6.12
text: Weighted kendall_tau(): tau-b denominator omitted double-tied pairs (ties_both) two tie-correction factors, deflating |tau| tied data. weighted denominator now mirrors unweighted (n0 - Tx - Txy)(n0 - Ty - Txy) structure. Weighted kruskal_wallis(): grand mean rank still hard-coded N/2 pre-0.6.4 rank convention instead (N+1)/2, inflating H. now derived weighted mid-ranks . bugs violated invariant weights exactly 1 must reproduce unweighted result. new package-wide invariance suite (tests/testthat/test-weights-invariance.R) enforces w == 1 reduction every weighted entry point; intentionally approximate reductions (design-based mann_whitney, weighted Kendall z/p) documented exceptions bounded assertions.

title: mariposa 0.6.12
text: README DESCRIPTION longer overclaim: paired t-test mode (yet implemented) longer advertised, “every function validated … results match” replaced Charter-compliant wording — validated SPSS v29 within documented per-tier tolerances, vignette("spss-compatibility") per-function status. weighted variants rank-based family — mann_whitney(), kruskal_wallis(), wilcoxon_test(), friedman_test(), binomial_test(), dunn_test(), pairwise_wilcoxon(), kendall_tau() — now disclosed R-(Tier 4) “Weighted variants” note help page: SPSS NPAR TESTS / NONPAR CORR ignore WEIGHT , SPSS reference exists weighted paths. mann_whitney()’s note also states design-based U/W may differ SPSS’s expanded-data U (Z p validated quantities); oneway_anova() now documents omega-/epsilon-squared truncated 0 (negative raw estimates occur F < 1).

title: mariposa 0.6.12
text: SPSS-compatibility vignette regenerated (frozen 2026-05-19) now carries “Internal (Tier 4)” marker weighted rank variants. Generator fixes: w_* family scheffe_test correctly matched shared test files (previously shown “validated” despite existing tests), zero-match tier counts longer report 1, assert_spss_count() call sites tallied Spec. test-t-test-spss-validation.R: header tier table claimed t-statistic Spec (±1e-5) assertions use Display(3); header now matches assertions. last expect_no_error() validation file (test-linear-regression-spss-validation.R) replaced real assertions per-group predictions; validation-discipline meta-test now passes MARIPOSA_VALIDATION_STRICT=TRUE.

title: mariposa 0.6.11
text: Deprecation cleanup (theme: due bridges come ). Two batches deprecations reached removal release together: 0.6.9 argument bridges (originally slated 0.6.10) 0.6.10 duplicate result columns. Removing keeps run-1.0 API freeze tidy.

title: mariposa 0.6.11
text: codebook(): show.id, show.type, show.labels, show.values, show.freq, show.na, show.unused, max.values, max.len, sort..name (use show_id, show_type, show_labels, show_values, show_freq, show_na, show_unused, max_values, max_len, sort_by_name) val_labels(): drop.na (use drop_na) drop_labels(): drop.na (use drop_na) bridges originally slated removal 0.6.10 batched release. frequency()/rec()/to_label() family removed-argument errors introduced 0.6.9 remain place permanent guidance (... consumes tidyselect, clear error beats silent misinterpretation). chisq_gof(), friedman_test(): chi_sq removed (use chi_squared) mcnemar_test(): statistic removed (use chi_squared) mann_whitney(): effect_size_r removed (use r_effect) oneway_anova(): F_stat removed (use F_statistic) statistical values unchanged; redundant column names go away.

title: mariposa 0.6.10
text: Result-column harmonization (theme: one statistic, one column name). style audit found statistic carrying different result-column names across sibling functions; drifted names now converge canonical spelling, old columns kept duplicates one release.

title: mariposa 0.6.10
text: Chi-square statistic: chi_squared (chi_square()) - now also chisq_gof(), friedman_test(), mcnemar_test() Effect size r: r_effect (wilcoxon_test()) - now also mann_whitney() F statistic: F_statistic (levene_test()) - now also oneway_anova() Print summary methods read canonical columns; statistical values unchanged.

title: mariposa 0.6.10
text: chisq_gof(), friedman_test(): chi_sq (use chi_squared) mcnemar_test(): statistic (use chi_squared) mann_whitney(): effect_size_r (use r_effect) oneway_anova(): F_stat (use F_statistic)

title: mariposa 0.6.9
text: API-cleanup completion (theme: 0.6.8 bridges come , last dot-case stragglers get ). One step closer 1.0 API freeze.

title: mariposa 0.6.9
text: frequency()/fre(): sort.frq, show.na, show.prc, show.valid, show.sum, show.labels, show.unused rec(): .factor, var.label, val.labels to_label()/to_character()/to_numeric(): drop.na, drop.unused, add.non.labelled, use.labels, start., keep.labels read_spss()/read_por()/read_stata()/read_sas()/read_xpt(): tag.na : frequency(data, x, sort.frq = "desc") warned worked. : errors pointer sort_frq. functions whose ... selects variables, old names raise clear “removed 0.6.9” error instead silently swallowed tidyselect; readers fail unused arguments.

title: mariposa 0.6.9
text: codebook(): show_id, show_type, show_labels, show_values, show_freq, show_na, show_unused, max_values, max_len, sort_by_name val_labels(): drop_na drop_labels(): drop_na display options stored codebook results (result$options) use snake_case keys well.

title: mariposa 0.6.8
text: API-unification release (theme: snake_case arguments). One release-long deprecation bridge per versioning policy - old names keep working warn per session; removed 0.6.9.

title: mariposa 0.6.8
text: frequency()/fre(): sort_frq, show_na, show_prc, show_valid, show_sum, show_labels, show_unused rec(): as_factor, var_label, val_labels to_label()/to_character()/to_numeric(): drop_na, drop_unused, add_non_labelled, use_labels, start_at, keep_labels read_spss()/read_por()/read_stata()/read_sas()/read_xpt(): tag_na Base-R-universal names (na.rm, conf.level, var.equal) kept.

title: mariposa 0.6.8
text: t_test() results longer carry duplicated CI_lower/CI_upper alias columns; conf_int_lower/conf_int_upper contract.

title: mariposa 0.6.8
text: sort_frq validated ("none"/"asc"/"desc") - typos used silently produce unsorted table; show_labels validates TRUE/FALSE/"auto" values clear error. Vignettes showcase new argument names.

title: mariposa 0.6.7
text: Output-layer release (theme: uniform three-layer output). Statistical results unchanged; changed results present .

title: mariposa 0.6.7
text: Every analysis class now follows documented pattern t_test chi_square pioneered: result prints compact overview (headline statistic, p-value, significance stars, one line per test), summary(result) carries full detailed output behind boolean section toggles. Newly migrated: kruskal_wallis, wilcoxon_test, friedman_test, binomial_test, fisher_test, chisq_gof, mcnemar_test, levene_test, tukey_test, scheffe_test, dunn_test, pairwise_wilcoxon, frequency, crosstab (describe already compact gained summary layer uniformity). Nothing removed - everything old print() showed summary(), verified line--line.

title: mariposa 0.6.7
text: One shared engine three correlation functions (pearson/spearman/kendall results verified byte-identical across 21 scenarios; ~500 lines removed). w_* factory now supports multi-value non-numeric statistics; w_quantile() w_modus() ordinary plugins instead pipeline reimplementations (~420 lines removed, results identical across 31 scenarios).

title: mariposa 0.6.6
text: Internal-architecture release (theme: shared cores formatting utilities). statistical results change; table rendering Tukey/Scheffe output now aligned uses SPSS-style p display. One home every weighted formula: new weighted-statistics kernel file backs describe(), frequency(), w_* functions rank tests; weighted variance formula previously existed six files. New internal output utilities (bordered table renderer, grouped-results iterator, unified number/p formatting) - building blocks print style guide documented; adoption started post-hoc tests. Tukey Scheffe now share one engine one print implementation (results verified byte-identical); t_test() oneway_anova() restructured 500-line nested-closure bodies short orchestrators file-level helpers (byte-identical results). Weights summarise() context captured quosures (enquo/eval_tidy) instead frame-walking; shared validators report errors user-facing call site. Documentation internals standardized @noRd (man/ shrinks ~120 internal pages).

title: mariposa 0.6.5
text: Housekeeping release (theme: package hygiene). statistical results change. Slimmer dependencies: removed unused tidyr import pruned unused importFrom entries. Error chains: failures inside grouped analyses re-thrown cli_abort(parent = ...) original condition preserved; haven requirement enforced one central guard reports calling function instead internal helper. Import internals: native-missing-value detection shared read_stata(), read_sas() read_xpt() now lives one helper instead three copies. Mechanical polish: remaining sapply() calls oldest files converted type-stable vapply(); pkgdown reference now lists phi(), cramers_v(), goodman_gamma(). Test suite: removed legacy tolerance registry contradicted Validation Charter longer used test.

title: mariposa 0.6.4
text: quality release. Following -depth internal review entire statistical codebase, version sharpens accuracy several statistics, makes package behave consistently across functions, adds dedicated regression-test suite (tests/testthat/test-audit-regressions.R) guarantees hold future releases. outputs change slightly result - every case toward standard reference implementations.

title: mariposa 0.6.4
text: Kendall’s tau-b significance test now agrees stats::cor.test() (SPSS formula) machine precision, noticeable heavily tied data binary variables. weighted Wilcoxon signed-rank test (pairwise_wilcoxon()) now uses frequency-expansion mid-ranks: integer weights statistic equals expanded-data Wilcoxon exactly, weights = 1 reproduces unweighted test. Displayed rank means weighted Kruskal-Wallis Dunn tests follow convention. Mann-Whitney asymptotic p-value now matches reported Z (follow SPSS convention without continuity correction). Regression degrees freedom now derived fitted model terms, improving results models dummy-coded factors interaction terms (weighted linear regression logistic omnibus test). Kruskal-Wallis effect size now correctly labelled: returned field epsilon_squared (previously named eta_squared).

title: mariposa 0.6.4
text: linear_regression() gains SPSS-style collinearity diagnostics (Tolerance VIF per model term), including collinearity toggle summary(). phi(), cramers_v(), goodman_gamma() now return requested effect size directly numeric value - convenient behavior names suggest. full test output, use chi_square(). weighted two-sample t_test() now honors var.equal primary result. oneway_anova() always reports classical Welch results (like SPSS ONEWAY), var.equal argument deprecated; ss_type factorial_anova()/ancova() likewise deprecated favor SPSS-standard Type III.

title: mariposa 0.6.4
text: One package-wide weights policy: invalid (negative) weights now rejected clear message every entry point, instead handled differently depending function. weighted median now always equals weighted 50th percentile, unweighted quantiles follow SPSS convention (Type 6/HAVERAGE) throughout. frequency() header statistics use formulas describe() w_* functions. Significance stars follow single boundary convention everywhere, matching printed legend.

title: mariposa 0.6.4
text: Correlation functions handle constant variables gracefully (NA instead error), frequency(show.unused = TRUE) works variables tagged via set_na()/read_spss(), frequency(sort.frq =) now sorts frequency monotone cumulative-percent column. write_spss() protects valid values many missing-value codes must consolidated range, explains . logistic_regression() surfaces separation convergence warnings ; post-hoc tests report computation carried instead skipping silently.

title: mariposa 0.6.4
text: Internal code paths consolidated (shared helpers weighted mid-ranks Wilcoxon core) substantial amount unused code removed, making codebase easier maintain. Grouped single-variable w_* results print statistics .

what: rec() reliably matches decimal single values
title: mariposa 0.6.3.2
text: Single-value recode rules now match decimal codes (e.g. "3.6=2") even stored value carries floating-point representation error. single-value comparison changed exact numeric equality (x == value) string comparison (.character(x) == .character(value)), rounds 15 significant digits thereby absorbs error. Reason: value 0.1 + 0.2 stored 0.30000000000000004, previous exact == test silently failed match rule "0.3=...". mirrors behaviour sjmisc::rec(), rec()’s string syntax modelled. Range rules already robust (use >=/<=) unchanged.

title: mariposa 0.6.3.1
text: Adds explicit tidy(), glance(), augment() methods linear_regression logistic_regression results, registered via standard s3_register() pattern (broom Suggests, hard dep). Reason: class(r) = c("linear_regression", "lm"), broom::tidy.lm() broom::glance.lm() dispatched expected, internally called summary(x) — (specialised summary.linear_regression() overriding summary.lm) returned mariposa SPSS-style summary instead lm summary broom needs. visible failures: broom::glance(r) raised object 'r.squared' found mariposa’s summary stores R_squared. broom::tidy(r, conf.int = TRUE) returned 4 columns (term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high) instead expected 6+ (term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value, conf.low, conf.high). new methods strip linear_regression / logistic_regression class delegating broom::tidy.lm / tidy.glm etc., inner summary() call dispatches summary.lm / summary.glm broom receives expected shape. user-facing summary(r) still returns mariposa’s SPSS-style output (specific method wins). Edge cases stay consistent rest lm-generic surface: broom::tidy() / glance() / augment() grouped pairwise result raise actionable error pointing lapply(r$groups, ...) use = "listwise". New tests test-broom-methods.R cover three tidiers regression types, plus grouped/pairwise error paths.

what: Behavior Change — regression results inherit from lm / glm
title: mariposa 0.6.3
text: linear_regression() logistic_regression() results now fitted lm / glm object (mariposa-specific tables attached additional slots), instead wrapping $model. base-R broom generics dispatch natively: Class hierarchy c("linear_regression", "lm") linear c("logistic_regression", "glm", "lm") logistic. summary(r) still returns SPSS-style mariposa summary (specific method wins); raw lm/glm summary call stats::summary.lm(r) / stats::summary.glm(r).
code: r <- linear_regression(survey_data, life_satisfaction ~ age + income) coef(r)                                 # named numeric vector predict(r, newdata = head(survey_data)) # works directly anova(r)                                # sequential SS table vcov(r); confint(r); residuals(r); fitted(r) broom::tidy(r); broom::glance(r); broom::augment(r)

previous_headings: Behavior Change — regression results inherit from lm / glm
title: mariposa 0.6.3
text: Two slots collided lm/glm conventions renamed: Migration: r$coefficients → r$coef_table (SPSS-style tibble) coef(r) (named numeric vector). r$anova → r$anova_table (SPSS-style overall-model ANOVA tibble) anova(r) (R’s per-term sequential SS table). r$model |> predict(...) → predict(r, ...) directly. r$model |> broom::tidy() → broom::tidy(r) directly.

previous_headings: Behavior Change — regression results inherit from lm / glm
title: mariposa 0.6.3
text: use = "pairwise": single fitted lm available, result custom list class "linear_regression" . predict()/anova() etc. raise informative error pointing use = "listwise". Grouped results (top-level): single model. predict()/anova() raise informative error pointing lapply(r$groups, predict, ...). r$groups[[]] lm-inheriting object, per-group generics work directly.

title: mariposa 0.6.3
text: New test block test-linear-regression-spss-validation.R verifies coef(), predict(), anova(), vcov(), confint(), residuals(), fitted(), formula(), nobs(), model.matrix() dispatch natively, plus grouped/pairwise error paths. 1184/1184 tests pass; R CMD check built tarball: Status OK.

what: linear_regression() and logistic_regression(): factor predictor handling
title: mariposa 0.6.2
text: regression functions now expose factors argument controlling factor predictors enter model. new default factors = "dummy" matches base R lm() / glm(): factor L levels expands L - 1 dummy contrasts via stats::model.matrix(). Previous versions silently coerced factor levels integer codes (SPSS ordinal--scale default) warning, surprised users relied standard R semantics. restore previous SPSS-style behavior, pass factors = "numeric" explicitly. mode emits one-line cli::cli_inform() listing coerced variables transparency. “numeric” mode required reproduce SPSS REGRESSION / LOGISTIC REGRESSION output factor predictors carry ordered meaning (e.g., 4-level education variable treated 1–4 ordinal scale). Behavioral consequences: model 3-level factor predictor previously returned one coefficient row now returns two dummy-contrast rows new default. pairwise missing handling (use = "pairwise"), factor predictors supported factors = "dummy"; function now errors actionable message pointing either factors = "numeric" use = "listwise". Migration: scripts depend old SPSS-style coercion set factors = "numeric" call site. cli_inform() message can silenced suppressMessages() desired.

title: mariposa 0.6.2
text: Two functions joined Charter §5.1 audit list (“unrounded sum(w)” weighted-statistics convention previously applied t_test, oneway_anova, levene_test): linear_regression(): weighted variance, SE, df, F, R², adjusted-R² now use unrounded sum(weights) throughout. Earlier versions used n_effective <- round(sum(w)) df MS calculations, producing systematic drift SPSS REGRESSION (~0.001 F, ~0.01 adj-R² typical weights). displayed N still round(sum(w)). logistic_regression(): pseudo-R² formulas (Cox & Snell, Nagelkerke, McFadden) now use unrounded sum(weights) exponential denominator. displayed N rounded classification counts remain integers. bug fixes; weighted results may shift slightly toward closer agreement SPSS v29.

title: mariposa 0.6.2
text: summary.linear_regression(descriptives = TRUE) now actually prints Descriptive Statistics table (Variable, Mean, SD, N). Previously parameter accepted documented “Reserved future use” produced output. compact print.linear_regression() longer crashes weighted models non-integer df: F-statistic line now rounds df display formatting %d. Roxygen examples summary.linear_regression (collinearity = FALSE) summary.logistic_regression (classification_table = FALSE) referenced parameters exist; corrected descriptives = FALSE classification = FALSE respectively.

title: mariposa 0.6.2
text: linear_regression SPSS validation test suite expanded 1 scenario (unweighted bivariate) 6 scenarios covering four Charter §8 quadrants — Tests 1a, 1c, 2a, 2c, 3a, 4a tests/spss_reference/outputs/linear_regression_output.txt. weighted scenarios (2a, 2c, 4a) verify Charter §5.1 fix . New behavioral tests cover factors argument (dummy expansion, numeric coercion, pairwise + dummy + factor error path). 222/222 assertions pass.

title: mariposa 0.6.1
text: Substantial hardening SPSS-compatibility test suite. 29 SPSS- validation test files rewritten new Validation Charter (see vignette("spss-compatibility")) defines tolerance tiers (Spec / Display / Exception / Internal), forbids inline tolerance literals, NA placeholders, expect_true(TRUE) reporting blocks, requires citation comments linking every reference value source line tests/spss_reference/outputs/. 1832+ passing assertions across 29 validation files, 0 failures. New tests/testthat/helper-validation-tolerances.R provides assert_spss() tol() helpers explicit tier semantics. New tests/testthat/test-validation-discipline.R meta-test lints validation files Charter-forbidden patterns. New vignettes/spss-compatibility.Rmd reports per-function validation status, auto-generated test suite. New CI workflow .github/workflows/strict-validation.yaml runs full suite strict-discipline mode release tags weekly.

title: mariposa 0.6.1
text: Three weighted statistical functions corrected use unrounded sum(w) per SPSS frequency-weights convention. Earlier versions rounded early produced systematic drift SPSS weighted scenarios. t_test(): weighted variance, SE, df calculations now use unrounded sum(w) (one-sample two-sample paths). Welch- Satterthwaite df now derived unrounded per-group weighted N. oneway_anova(): weighted variance divisor now (sum(w) - 1) (sample formula, population). Weighted SE uses sqrt(sum(w)), sqrt(physical n). Weighted CI t-critical-value uses df = sum(w) - 1, Kish design-effective N. df_within now uses floor(sum(w)) - k (SPSS ONEWAY-specific convention). levene_test(): weighted Levene df now uses unrounded sum(w) - k (SPSS T-TEST family convention). changes bug fixes may slightly shift weighted-scenario results user code. Differences small (typically < 0.01 F t) bring mariposa closer agreement SPSS v29.

title: mariposa 0.6.1
text: vignette("spss-compatibility") documents per-function validation status, four tolerance tiers, SPSS-procedure-specific WEIGHT conventions discovered migration: T-TEST family: unrounded sum(w) ONEWAY: floor(sum(w)) UNIANOVA: Type III SS NPAR TESTS: WEIGHT effectively ignored NONPAR CORR (Spearman, Kendall): WEIGHT effectively ignored CORRELATIONS (Pearson): WEIGHT honored CROSSTABS, FREQUENCIES, RELIABILITY, FACTOR, REGRESSION: WEIGHT honored

title: mariposa 0.6.1
text: Title shortened “SPSS-Compatible Statistical Tools Survey Data” (CRAN soft-limit compliance). Suggests cleanup: removed PMCMRplus survey (longer needed).

title: mariposa 0.6.1
text: second audit pass identified additional math defects test fudges, corrected release: dunn_test(): SE now includes Dunn (1964) / Conover (1999) tie correction. Previous versions systematically -estimated |Z| tied data (e.g., Likert scales). Baselines regenerated PMCMRplus::kwAllPairsDunnTest (exact match 4 decimals). friedman_test(): weighted branch now applies tie correction consistently stats::friedman.test (unweighted branch). inconsistency caused weighted chi-squared values low tied data. describe(): weighted skewness kurtosis now delegate .calc_skewness() / .calc_kurtosis() helpers.R (Joanes-Gill Type-2 Σw substitution), matching w_skew() / w_kurtosis() SPSS FREQUENCIES exactly. previous duplicate implementation used simple weighted moment without bias correction. .w_quantile(): weighted quantiles now use Type-6 (HAVERAGE) linear interpolation cumulative-weight crossings — matches SPSS FREQUENCIES /PERCENTILES. Unweighted quantiles also switched R default type = 7 SPSS-compatible type = 6.

title: mariposa 0.6.1
text: Several SPSS-compatibility claims narrowed reflect code actually : Source comments test-file headers weighted paths kruskal_wallis(), wilcoxon_test(), friedman_test() corrected “design-based” / “Lumley-Scott” “frequency-weighted approximation”. mann_whitney() genuine Lumley & Scott (2013) implementation; others substitute sum(w) n standard variance formula. mann_whitney() test now includes permanent cross-check survey::svyranktest() (skipped survey installed). spearman_rho(): weights parameter docstring rewritten disclose weights used case filtering (per SPSS NONPAR CORR convention), rank correlation . pearson_cor(): docstring now warns weighted-df convention (n = sum(w)) gives spuriously narrow CIs raw expansion weights; users weights normalize first. logistic_regression(): test file replaced property-based assertions (Wald formula, Sig chi-sq, exp(B) vs independent 2x2 odds ratio, Cox & Snell/Nagelkerke/McFadden textbook formulas, Omnibus likelihood ratio). longer tautological glm-vs-glm self-comparison.

title: mariposa 0.6.1
text: oneway_anova(): removed dead-code overwrite grand_mean_welch weighted Welch path. levene_test(): stale comment claiming df2 = floor(sum(w)) - k corrected — code uses unrounded sum(w) - k (T-TEST family convention).

title: mariposa 0.6.0
text: release adds 10 label management functions working labelled survey data (inspired sjlabelled, consolidated clean, consistent API), plus data transformation, row operations, data exploration functions.

title: mariposa 0.6.0
text: New var_label(): dual-mode function getting setting variable labels. var_label(data) returns variable labels named character vector; var_label(data, x = "Age", y = "Gender") sets labels specific columns. Supports tidyselect column selection getting labels. New val_labels(): dual-mode function getting setting value labels. val_labels(data) returns value labels named list; val_labels(data, x = c("Low" = 1, "High" = 2)) sets labels. Use .add = TRUE extend existing labels without replacing . New copy_labels(): copies label attributes (variable labels, value labels, class, tagged NA metadata) source data frame matching columns target. Essential preserving labels dplyr operations strip attributes. New drop_labels(): removes value labels values actually occur data. Use drop.na = TRUE also remove labels tagged NA values.

title: mariposa 0.6.0
text: New to_label(): converts haven_labelled vectors factors, using value labels factor levels. Supports ordered, drop.na, drop.unused, add.non.labelled options. Factor levels ordered original numeric codes (alphabetically). New to_character(): converts haven_labelled vectors character, replacing numeric codes label text. New to_numeric(): converts factors labelled vectors numeric. use.labels = TRUE, uses value labels numeric; otherwise assigns sequential integers (controlled start.). New to_labelled(): converts factors, character, numeric vectors haven_labelled proper value labels. Factor levels become value labels automatically.

title: mariposa 0.6.0
text: New set_na(): declares specific numeric values missing (NA tagged NA). Supports unnamed values (applied numeric columns) named pairs per-variable control (e.g., set_na(data, income = c(-9, -8))). tag = TRUE (default), creates tagged NAs integrate na_frequencies(), frequency(), codebook(). Can called incrementally add new missing value codes. New unlabel(): strips label metadata variables, converting haven_labelled vectors plain base R types. Removes variable labels, value labels, tagged NA metadata, format attributes. Tagged NAs become regular NA. Supports tidyselect selective column unlabelling.

title: mariposa 0.6.0
text: New rec(): flexible recoding string syntax (e.g., rec(data, x, rec = "1:2=1 [Low]; 3:5=2 [High]")). Supports value ranges, min/max keywords, copy unchanged values, automatic value label generation bracket syntax. Works numeric, character, labelled vectors. New to_dummy(): creates dummy (indicator) variables categorical labelled vectors. Generates one 0/1 column per unique value informative column names. Supports tidyselect multi-variable dummy coding suffix = "label" use value labels column names. New std(): z-standardization four methods ("sd", "2sd", "mad", "gmd"). Supports survey weights, grouped standardization via dplyr::group_by(), robust = TRUE median/MAD-based standardization. New center(): mean-centering (grand-mean group-mean). Supports survey weights dplyr::group_by() group-mean centering. Returns centered values centering value stored attribute.

title: mariposa 0.6.0
text: New row_means(): computes row-wise means across selected columns, min_valid parameter matching SPSS MEAN.x() syntax. Designed use inside dplyr::mutate(). Replaces deprecated scale_index(). New row_sums(): computes row-wise sums across selected columns, min_valid parameter minimum valid (non-NA) values. New row_count(): counts occurrences specific values per row. Useful counting endorsements multi-item scales (e.g., many items respondent agreed ).

title: mariposa 0.6.0
text: New find_var(): searches variables name label using regular expressions. Returns matching variable names labels. Useful exploring large survey datasets many variables.

title: mariposa 0.6.0
text: scale_index() removed replaced row_means(), provides functionality clearer name. Update existing code: scale_index(data, x, y, z) → row_means(data, x, y, z).

title: mariposa 0.5.6
text: New write_spss() function: exports data frames SPSS .sav format full tagged NA roundtripping. Tagged NAs converted back SPSS user-defined missing values, enabling lossless roundtrips via read_spss() -> processing -> write_spss(). Supports byte, none, zsav compression. New write_stata() function: exports data frames Stata .dta format. Tagged NAs source format written Stata extended missing values (..z). Supports Stata versions 8-15. New write_xpt() function: exports data frames SAS transport .xpt format. Tagged NAs written SAS special missing values (..Z, ._). Supports transport versions 5 8.

title: mariposa 0.5.6
text: Import: read_spss(), read_por(), read_stata(), read_sas(), read_xpt(), read_xlsx() Export: write_spss(), write_stata(), write_xpt(), write_xlsx() Cross-format export supported: data imported one format can exported another (e.g., SPSS Stata) automatic missing value type conversion.

title: mariposa 0.5.5
text: Auto-detects mariposa export format (data frame, list, codebook) Reconstructs haven_labelled columns, factor levels, variable labels Restores tagged NAs na_tag_map missing codes data Works plain Excel reader non-mariposa files write_xlsx(data, "file.xlsx") – data + “Labels” reference sheet variable labels, value labels, missing value codes codebook(data) |> write_xlsx("codebook.xlsx") – structured codebook workbook Overview, Codebook, optional per-variable frequency sheets (frequencies = TRUE) write_xlsx(list(= df1, b = df2), "multi.xlsx") – multi-sheet export named list element becomes sheet

title: mariposa 0.5.5
text: write_xlsx() now preserves tagged NA codes (-9, -11, etc.) visible values data sheet instead empty cells, enabling perfect roundtripping read_xlsx(). System NAs remain empty cells. “Labels” sheet now includes Column_Type column (haven_labelled factor) read_xlsx() can deterministically reconstruct column types.

title: mariposa 0.5.5
text: Added openxlsx2 suggested dependency Excel import/export.

title: mariposa 0.5.4
text: New read_stata() function: reads Stata .dta files annotates native extended missing values (..z) use mariposa’s tagged NA system. Stata tagged NAs preserved automatically haven; read_stata() adds na_tag_map attribute seamless integration na_frequencies(), frequency(), codebook(). New read_sas() function: reads SAS .sas7bdat files optional catalog file (.sas7bcat) value labels. Annotates SAS special missing values (..Z ._) tagged NA integration. New read_xpt() function: reads SAS transport files (.xpt) tagged missing value support. Transport files FDA-approved, platform-independent SAS data format. New read_por() function: reads SPSS portable .por files tagged NA support read_spss(). Shares SPSS missing value conversion logic internally.

title: mariposa 0.5.4
text: na_frequencies() column spss_code renamed code reflect multi-format support. column now contains character values: numeric SPSS codes (e.g., "-9") native format codes (e.g., "." Stata, "." SAS).

title: mariposa 0.5.4
text: na_frequencies(), untag_na(), strip_tags() now work universally data supported formats (SPSS, Stata, SAS). untag_na() now format-aware: Stata SAS data (tagged NAs native representation numeric codes recover), warns falls back strip_tags() behavior. frequency() codebook() automatically display format-appropriate missing value codes (e.g., -9 SPSS, .Stata, .SAS).

title: mariposa 0.5.3
text: New read_spss() function: reads SPSS .sav files preserves user-defined missing values tagged NAs instead converting regular NA. allows distinguishing different types missing data (e.g., “answer”, “applicable”, “refused”) still treating NA standard R operations. Fixes sjlabelled::read_spss(tag.na=TRUE) crash large datasets (e.g., ALLBUS) caused --bounds letters[] indexing. New na_frequencies() function: shows breakdown different types missing values tagged NA variable, counts, original SPSS codes, value labels. New untag_na() function: converts tagged NAs back original SPSS missing value codes (e.g., -9, -8, -42). New strip_tags() function: converts tagged NAs regular (untagged) NA values, producing result reading haven::read_sav() directly.

title: mariposa 0.5.3
text: frequency() now displays tagged NAs individually data imported read_spss(). missing value type shown separate row original SPSS code label, followed “Total Valid” “Total Missing” summary row. frequency() show.unused = TRUE correctly handles tagged NA labels (longer shows unused freq=0).

title: mariposa 0.5.2.2
text: New fre() shorthand alias frequency(). functions identical; fre() simply provides quicker way call frequency analysis. ?fre shows help page ?frequency.

title: mariposa 0.5.2
text: New codebook() function: generates interactive HTML data dictionary displayed RStudio Viewer pane. Shows variable ID, name, type, label, empirical values, value labels, frequencies clean, scrollable table. Inspired sjPlot’s view_df() built natively htmltools. HTML codebook features subtle-accent design: dark header, alternating row stripes, monospace type badges, per-value frequency counts displayed vertical lists aligned across columns. Console print() shows minimal metadata overview (variable count, observations, types). Full details reserved HTML viewer. summary() method provides detailed text-based fallback toggleable sections (overview, variable_details, value_labels). Supports tidyselect variable selection, optional survey weights weighted frequencies, sort..name ordering.

title: mariposa 0.5.2
text: Added htmltools imported dependency HTML codebook generation.

title: mariposa 0.5.1
text: 13 analysis functions now support summary() detailed SPSS-style output toggleable sections. three-layer pattern works follows: print() — compact one-line overview (default typing object name) summary() — builds detailed summary object boolean section toggles print.summary() — renders full verbose output requested sections Supported functions: t_test(), oneway_anova(), factorial_anova(), ancova(), chi_square(), mann_whitney(), pearson_cor(), spearman_rho(), kendall_tau(), reliability(), efa(), linear_regression(), logistic_regression(). summary() method accepts boolean parameters control output sections displayed (e.g., summary(result, effect_sizes = FALSE) summary(result, descriptives = FALSE)).

title: mariposa 0.5.1
text: Added build_summary_object() format_p_compact() R/summary_helpers.R shared infrastructure summary methods.

title: mariposa 0.5.1
text: Complete Roxygen2 documentation 39 S3 methods (13 print + 13 summary 13 print.summary), @description, @param, @return, @examples, @seealso. 13 main function @examples now demonstrate three-layer output pattern (result, summary(result), summary(result, toggle = FALSE)). Added print.reliability() print.efa() documentation (previously undocumented).

title: mariposa 0.5.1
text: Fixed print.chi_square() Roxygen2 tag (@keywords internal replaced correct @method print chi_square). Fixed example syntax errors ancova() factorial_anova() (formula syntax replaced correct dv/interface). Fixed incorrect variable name education_level examples (corrected education).

title: mariposa 0.5.1
text: Added test-summary-methods.R tests 13 summary methods. Updated test-print-methods.R reflect new three-layer structure.

title: mariposa 0.5.0
text: Added factorial_anova() multi-factor -subjects ANOVA (3 factors) Type III Sum Squares matching SPSS UNIANOVA. Includes main effects, interaction terms, partial eta squared, R-squared, Levene’s test homogeneity variance. Full survey weight support via WLS (matching SPSS /REGWGT). Integrates existing tukey_test(), scheffe_test(), levene_test() S3 generics. Added ancova() Analysis Covariance — tests group differences controlling continuous covariates. Matches SPSS UNIANOVA keyword. Provides ANOVA table, parameter estimates (B, SE, t, p, partial eta squared), estimated marginal means (adjusted covariates), Levene’s test. Supports 3 factors multiple covariates full survey weight support.

title: mariposa 0.5.0
text: Added 612 SPSS validation tests factorial_anova() across 9 scenarios: unweighted (2-factor, 3-factor, 2-factor missing data), weighted (2-factor, 3-factor), grouped (2-factor, 3-factor), weighted+grouped (2-factor, 3-factor). Added 579 SPSS validation tests ancova() across 11 scenarios: one-way ANCOVA, two-way ANCOVA, weighted, grouped, weighted+grouped, multiple covariates, single factor single covariate. Total test suite: 4,986 tests passing (0 failures).

title: mariposa 0.5.0
text: Type III Sum Squares computed via contr.sum contrasts stats::drop1() — dependency car package. Weighted analyses use WLS (stats::lm() weights), matching SPSS’s /REGWGT subcommand behavior exactly. Weighted Levene’s test uses SPSS /REGWGT algorithm: z_i = sqrt(w_i) * |y_i - weighted_cell_mean_i| followed unweighted ANOVA. Corrected Model SS computed Corrected Total - Error (sum Type III SS) correctly handle unbalanced designs.

title: mariposa 0.4.0
text: Added fisher_test() Fisher’s exact test independence contingency tables. Recommended sample sizes small expected cell frequencies fall 5 (chi-square approximation becomes unreliable). Supports survey weights, multi-variable analysis, group_by(). Added chisq_gof() chi-square goodness--fit testing. Tests whether observed frequency distribution categorical variable matches expected distribution (default: equal proportions). Supports custom expected proportions, residual analysis, survey weights, multi-variable analysis. Added mcnemar_test() testing changes paired proportions two dichotomous measurements (e.g., /designs). Provides asymptotic exact binomial p-values, 2×2 contingency tables, continuity correction. Supports survey weights. Added dunn_test() S3 generic Dunn’s post-hoc pairwise comparisons following significant Kruskal-Wallis test. Identifies specific group pairs differ using rank-based Z-statistics adjustable p-value correction (Bonferroni, Holm, BH, etc.). Dispatches kruskal_wallis result objects. Added pairwise_wilcoxon() S3 generic pairwise Wilcoxon signed-rank post-hoc comparisons following significant Friedman test. Identifies measurement pairs differ adjustable p-value correction. Dispatches friedman_test result objects.

title: mariposa 0.4.0
text: Added SPSS validation tests 5 new functions across weighted/unweighted grouped/ungrouped scenarios.

title: mariposa 0.4.0
text: Extended post-hoc analysis framework: dunn_test() pairwise_wilcoxon() join tukey_test(), scheffe_test(), levene_test() S3 generics dispatch parent test result objects.

title: mariposa 0.3.1
text: efa() now supports Maximum Likelihood (ML) extraction via extraction = "ml". ML extraction provides goodness--fit chi-square test, initial communalities SMC (squared multiple correlations), uniquenesses. Uses stats::factanal() correlation matrix input seamless survey weight support. efa() now supports Promax rotation via rotation = "promax". Like Oblimin, Promax oblique rotation produces Pattern Matrix, Structure Matrix, Factor Correlation Matrix. Uses stats::promax() (base R, new dependency). Internal refactoring efa(): extraction logic separated .efa_extract_pca() .efa_extract_ml() cleaner architecture easier extension future extraction methods (PAF planned).

title: mariposa 0.3.0
text: Added kruskal_wallis() comparing 3+ independent groups ordinal data (non-parametric alternative one-way ANOVA). Supports survey weights, group_by(), multi-variable analysis. Effect size: Eta-squared. Added wilcoxon_test() comparing two paired measurements without assuming normality (Wilcoxon signed-rank test). Includes rank categories (negative, positive, ties) effect size r. Added friedman_test() comparing 3+ related measurements ordinal data (non-parametric alternative repeated-measures ANOVA). Effect size: Kendall’s W. Added binomial_test() testing whether observed proportion matches expected value (exact binomial test). Supports multiple binary variables custom test proportions.

title: mariposa 0.3.0
text: Added 294 new SPSS validation tests across 4 non-parametric functions, covering weighted/unweighted grouped/ungrouped scenarios. Total test suite: 2,227 tests passing (0 failures, 0 skips).

title: mariposa 0.2.0
text: Added reliability() Cronbach’s Alpha item statistics, including corrected item-total correlations, alpha--item-deleted, inter-item correlation matrix. Genuine implementation full survey weight support. Added efa() Exploratory Factor Analysis PCA extraction. Supports Varimax rotation (Base R) Oblimin rotation (via optional GPArotation package). Includes KMO measure, Bartlett’s test, communalities, sorted factor loading matrix configurable blank threshold. Added scale_index() creating mean indices across survey items, min_valid parameter matching SPSS MEAN.x() syntax. Designed use inside dplyr::mutate(). Added pomps() Percent Maximum Possible Scores transformation, rescaling values 0-100 range cross-scale comparability. Added linear_regression() wrapper around stats::lm() SPSS-compatible output: coefficients table (B, SE, Beta, t, p), ANOVA table, model summary (R, R-squared, adjusted R-squared), standardized coefficients. Supports formula SPSS-style (dependent/predictors) interfaces. Added logistic_regression() wrapper around stats::glm() odds ratios, Wald statistics, pseudo-R-squared measures (Nagelkerke, Cox-Snell, McFadden), classification table.

title: mariposa 0.2.0
text: Added GPArotation suggested dependency Oblimin rotation efa(). Added MASS suggested dependency enhanced regression diagnostics.

title: mariposa 0.2.0
text: 6 new functions support survey weights grouped analysis via dplyr::group_by(). functions include comprehensive roxygen2 documentation practical examples, “Use” guidance, “Understanding Output” sections.

title: mariposa 0.1.0
text: gamma() renamed goodman_gamma() avoid shadowing base::gamma(). function remains alias chi_square() works identically. S3 class names unified: removed _results suffix result classes (e.g., chi_square_results -> chi_square, t_test_results -> t_test). Class names now match function name created .

title: mariposa 0.1.0
text: Fixed namespace collisions triple-defined internal helper functions (.process_variables(), .process_weights(), .effective_n()). now defined helpers.R shared across functions. Fixed weighted variance/SD formula inconsistency. weighted calculations now use SPSS frequency weights formula: sum(w * (x - w_mean)^2) / (V1 - 1). Fixed Gamma ASE (asymptotic standard error) calculation. Replaced empirical magic-number formula correct ASE0 formula Agresti (2002). Fixed weighted Cohen’s d calculation t_test(). Previously multiplied values weights (x * w); now uses proper weighted means pooled weighted standard deviation. Fixed weighted kurtosis formula. Changed population excess kurtosis (m4/m2^2 - 3) SPSS Type 2 sample-corrected formula (G2 = ((n+1)*g2 + 6) * (n-1) / ((n-2)*(n-3))), matching SPSS output.

title: mariposa 0.1.0
text: Refactored 9 11 w_* functions use shared factory pattern (R/w_factory.R). Eliminated ~2,460 lines duplicated boilerplate (4,204 → 1,740 lines, -58.6%). w_modus w_quantile remain standalone due fundamentally different interfaces. Added 83 SPSS validation tests w_* functions across 4 scenarios (weighted/unweighted × grouped/ungrouped) test-weighted-statistics-spss-validation.R. Reduced memory usage: result objects now store columns needed post-hoc tests instead full input data frame. Unified print helper system: output formatting now uses print_helpers.R. Removed deprecated .print_header(), .print_border(), .get_border(), .print_group_header() helpers.R. Deduplicated t_test.R print methods: print.t_test_results print.t_test_result now share common implementation (~360 fewer lines). Added input validation: t_test(): validates conf.level 0 1 t_test(), oneway_anova(): validate selected variables numeric chi_square(): warns expected cell counts < 5 Migrated error handling stop()/warning() cli_abort()/cli_warn() structured messages, {.arg} {.var} markup, pluralization support. Added cli dependency professional user-facing messages print output. Added @family tags 24 exported functions cross-referencing documentation (families: descriptive, hypothesis_tests, correlation, posthoc, weighted_statistics). Added tests/testthat/helper-mariposa.R shared test utilities centralized SPSS validation tolerances. Migrated print_helpers.R infrastructure cli (cli_rule(), cli_bullets(), cli_h2()). Extended globals.R missing NSE variable declarations. Fixed “SURVEYSTAT” reference imports.R.
